name: i_sheets_partial_identity_sale_not_fabricated
notes: Grounds VT-209's canonical-field set (canonical_fields.py: customer_name/phone are identity fields with rich GLOBAL_FIELD_HINTS aliases; order_amount/order_date are separate sale fields) at the conversational layer: when the owner explicitly states their sheet carries ONLY identity columns, the agent must not later claim to have pulled/mapped sale data that was never described. Setup: `setup --onboarded` then `script <tenant> i_sheets_partial_identity_sale_not_fabricated.json`.

steps.message: My Google Sheet only has two columns — Customer Name and Mobile Number. No order amounts or order dates in it at all.
steps.note: Owner states an identity-only sheet (name+phone map cleanly to customer_name/phone). No sale fields exist — the agent must not assume/fabricate their presence.

steps.message: Can you also pull in my order amounts and order dates from that same sheet as well?
steps.note: Owner asks for sale-field data the sheet doesn't have (per their own prior turn) — the agent must not fabricate having found/mapped order_amount or order_date; an honest 'that column isn't in your sheet' class reply is expected.
